name: auditd
title: auditd
summary: User space tools for security auditing
description:
User space tools for security auditing
This package contains the user space utilities for storing and searching the
audit records generated by the audit subsystem in the Linux kernel.
.
Also contains the audit dispatcher "audisp".

arguments.description: Write raw audit records used to create the displayed report into a file aulast.log in
arguments.raw: --extract  Write raw audit records used to create the displayed report into a file aulast.log in

arguments.description: out the audit event serial numbers used to determine the preceding line of the
arguments.raw: --proof  out the audit event serial numbers used to determine the preceding line of the

arguments.description: Take audit records from stdin. The audit events must be in the raw format.
arguments.raw: --stdin  Take audit records from stdin. The audit events must be in the raw format.

commands.name: ausyscall
commands.description: a program that allows mapping syscall names and numbers

commands.name: audisp-af_unix
commands.description: plugin to push audit events to an af_unix socket

commands.name: audisp-filter
commands.description: plugin to filter audit events and forward them to other plugins

commands.name: audisp-syslog
commands.description: plugin to push audit events into syslog

commands.name: auditctl
commands.description: a utility to assist controlling the kernel's audit system

commands.name: augenrules
commands.description: a script that merges component audit rule files

commands.name: aureport
commands.description: a tool that produces summary reports of audit daemon logs

commands.name: ausearch
commands.description: a tool to query audit daemon logs

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aulast because User space tools for security auditing.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aulast as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aulast [ options ]` as documented upstream for aulast.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ausearch --start this-month --raw | aulast --stdin` as documented upstream for aulast.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aulast with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aulast --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
